title: Configure the recovery period for dropped managed tables (Public Preview) ​
body:
**June 15, 2026**

You can now configure the recovery period for dropped Unity Catalog managed tables. By default, tables are recoverable for 7 days after being dropped using the [UNDROP TABLE](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-syntax-ddl-undrop-table) command. You can set the recovery period to 0 hours (to immediately purge dropped data) or between 7–30 days at the catalog or schema level. A longer period provides added protection against accidental drops of critical data, while a shorter period reduces cloud storage costs for workloads that frequently create and drop tables. Requires Databricks Runtime 17.3 and above. See [Configure the recovery period](/aws/en/tables/managed#configure-the-recovery-period).
